user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: tools/graphics_converter.py
## Active selection of the file:
The source directory is `gfx-assets\sprites`
The goal is to create RGB565 arrays inside `src\sprites.cpp` for every image file inside `gfx-assets\sprites`, there will be a list available in `inc\sprites.h` with `extern` for each.
Each array is a uint16_t array, with attributes like `const` so it lives in flash memory
The index 0 element of the array is a image width, the index 1 element is the image height, the bitmap payload follows
The name of the array starts with `sprit_` and ends with whatever the file name is, without extension, and all illegal characters transformed to `_`, and do not allow consecutive `_` or trailing `_`.
If the file name ends with ".fw.png", ignore the `fw`.
If there is alpha transparency, assume the background will be black
## My request for Codex:
implement the highlighted python script description
agent_reply: Implemented [tools/graphics_converter.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/graphics_converter.py). I also ran it once successfully:
```text
generated 1 sprite(s)
wrote inc\sprites.h
wrote src\sprites.cpp
Current generated symbol is `sprit_splash`, from `splash.fw.png`. Python syntax check passed too.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: tools/graphics_converter.py
## Active selection of the file:
The source directory is `gfx-assets\sprites`
The goal is to create uint8_t arrays inside `src\sprites.cpp` for every image file inside `gfx-assets\sprites`, there will be a list available in `inc\sprites.h` with `extern` for each.
only PNG and JPG will be supported
Open the file, for PNGs, make sure it is losslessly compressed and have no more meta data, use a temp file if needed. Then the binary array is simply the full file contents.
For JPG, just make sure it has no metadata, and again, the byte array is just the file contents

The idea is to use `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_pngle.c` and `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_tjpgd.c` directly without using a file-system, and also have constants to know how to make the sprites in-memory
Each array is a uint8_t array, with attributes like `const` so it lives in flash memory, maybe add `PROGMEM`?
The name of the array starts with `sprit_` and ends with whatever the file name is, without extension, and all illegal characters transformed to `_`, and do not allow consecutive `_` or trailing `_`.
If the file name ends with ".fw.png", ignore the `fw`.
Then also create constants for each array in the header file, all caps, ending with `_WIDTH` and `_HEIGHT` and `_BYTES`
## My request for Codex:
that was a bad idea, the image was huge and uncompressed
the better idea, implement what I am highlighting
agent_reply: Implemented the new compressed-byte-array converter in [tools/graphics_converter.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/graphics_converter.py).

It now only accepts PNG/JPG/JPEG, strips metadata, recompresses PNG losslessly, emits `const uint8_t ...[] PROGMEM` arrays in [src/sprites.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/sprites.cpp), and writes externs plus `*_WIDTH`, `*_HEIGHT`, and `*_BYTES` macros in [inc/sprites.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/sprites.h).
Current generated result:
```text
gfx-assets\sprites\splash.fw.png -> sprit_splash 320x240, 41821 bytes
That is a much healthier firmware asset than the raw RGB565 array. `pio run -e theflybringuptest` passes.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode
more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: platformio.ini
## My request for Codex:
that went acceptably well
but you swapped my memo button with a mic button, that's wrong
also, now that the icons are smaller, it shows that the unload function for the splash screen does not clear the screen, so it shows up through the cracks when mainscreenview is loaded
the same problem is happening when exiting out of scrollview, the old exit door graphic is slipping through the crack
I believe we solved this twice with drawing a gigantic black rectangle in the unload function
agent_reply: Fixed both issues.

The main memo button now uses the proper generated `sprit_memo_100` asset in [MainScreenView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:49). I copied `memo_100.png` into `gfx-assets/sprites/` and regenerated `inc/sprites.h` / `src/sprites.cpp`, so the symbol exists cleanly.
I also added unload clears:
- [SplashView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:15) now clears the full screen on unload.

- [ScrollView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:184) now clears the content area below the top bar on unload, which should stop the old exit-door pixels showing through.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly` succeeded. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` hash afterward, because that file keeps begging us to leave it alone.

commit_message: bt host delete, debug shown on top bar
recording works great now, tweaked the ui
more sprites, including overlays, goes along with icon rewrite
